slides.title: You're not eating *enough fiber*.
slides.sub: Most people get half of what they need — and it's quietly behind the hunger and bloat.

slides.title: Fiber keeps you full
slides.body: Fiber *slows digestion*, so you stay full longer and snack less without trying.

slides.title: Aim for 25–35g
slides.body: Most people get *half that*. Closing the gap changes your hunger and energy fast.

slides.title: Eat the whole thing
slides.body: Skip the juice, eat the *whole fruit*. The skin and pulp are where the fiber lives.

slides.title: Add beans and oats
slides.body: *Beans, oats and lentils* are the cheapest fiber there is. One serving moves the needle.

slides.title: Ramp up gradually
slides.body: Add fiber *slowly, with water*, or your gut will protest. Give it a week to adjust.

slides.title: Full longer, *effortlessly*.
slides.body: Vibe Health breaks down your meals so you can see your fiber — not just calories — and actually hit it.

caption:
Most people eat half the fiber they need and wonder why they're always snacky. 🌾

Eat whole fruit, add beans and oats, ramp up slowly. It's the most underrated fullness hack there is. Save this. 🔖

Vibe Health shows your fiber, not just your calories → link in bio.
youtube_title: Why You're Not Eating Enough Fiber
